title: --userns=keep-id doesn't work with --init
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`podman run` failed if both `--userns=keep-id` and `--init` are supplied

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start with a fresh updated Fedora 35 machine

2. `podman run -it --userns=keep-id --init ubuntu:latest`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: executable file `/dev/init` not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container starts

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
- Using *only* `--init` or *only* `--userns=keep-id` works
- This is a regression, this used to work until a week or so
- Works with `sudo` (maybe because `keep-id` becomes a no-op?)

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 23:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes. This is the latest version Fedora ships. If there is a way to try a later version please let me know.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Reproduced on my physical machine and with a fresh install on GNOME Boxes.

comments.body:
that is a regression in crun: https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/890

I'll cut a new crun release this week
